default_action: Provide home care guidance and routine follow-up.

rules.recommended_action: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic. Refer urgently to hospital.
rules.reason: Presence of a general danger sign, stridor, or chest indrawing indicates severe pneumonia or very severe disease.

rules.recommended_action: Give oral amoxicillin for 5 days. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 2 days.
rules.reason: Fast breathing in a child with cough indicates pneumonia.

symptomsAny: skin pinch goes back very slowly
rules.recommended_action: Give fluid for severe dehydration (Plan C). Refer urgently to hospital.
rules.reason: Two or more signs including sunken eyes or lethargy indicates severe dehydration.

rules.recommended_action: Give fluid, zinc supplements, and food for some dehydration (Plan B). Follow up in 5 days.
rules.reason: Two or more signs like restlessness or thirst indicates some dehydration.

rules.recommended_action: Give first dose of antimalarial. Treat to prevent low blood sugar. Refer urgently.
rules.reason: Fever with danger signs or stiff neck in malaria area is very severe febrile disease.

rules.recommended_action: Give recommended first-line oral antimalarial. Give paracetamol for high fever.
Soothe throat and relieve cough. Advise when to return. Follow up in 5 days.
rules.reason: Cough without fast breathing or danger signs is likely a simple cold.
